title: Schweiggeria pauciflora
intro: Schweiggeria pauciflora, commonly known as the swamp clematis or clematis, is a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Schweiggeria pauciflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.

The genus Schweiggeria is further divided into multiple species, with Schweiggeria pauciflora being one of the many species within this genus. Schweiggeria pauciflora's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
